stage.name: Add the second real asset producer
stage.targetUser: Solo and small-team 2D game developers who need coherent 32px ground tiles alongside their existing character work
stage.qualityGate: A developer can create, paint, theme, save, transfer, and export a deterministic cardinal terrain tileset from the same local-first project without changing existing character semantics or requiring a server

promises.statement: A developer can create a coherent 32px terrain tileset without drawing every edge and corner manually.
promises.context: The project already contains or may later contain a character and shared visual theme
promises.trigger: They open Terrain and choose a ground material
promises.outcome: The product deterministically generates all sixteen cardinal-neighbor tiles and a live paintable map preview
promises.quality: Every mask is present, seams are deterministic, previews update within one frame for ordinary maps, and identical documents produce identical pixels
promises.recovery: Invalid or incomplete terrain state remains editable, names exact blockers, and never corrupts the character recipe
promises.evidence: Pure mask/render fixtures plus actual-UI creation, painting, theming, and recovery flows pass on web and packaged Electron

promises.statement: A developer can keep terrain and character work in one local-first project while each producer retains its own versioned document.
promises.user: Returning developer alternating between character and terrain work
promises.context: One project may contain a verified character recipe and one optional terrain document
promises.trigger: They save, reopen, snapshot, copy, import, or recover the project
promises.outcome: Both producers preserve exact semantic state and use one project identity, revision, storage lifecycle, and portable backup
promises.quality: Character-only project bytes and render/export hashes remain unchanged; terrain transactions use the existing optimistic revision and recovery model
promises.recovery: Old readers reject terrain archives before mutation; conflicts, invalid documents, and interrupted writes preserve the last committed graph
promises.evidence: Repository, archive-v3, migration, conflict, snapshot, copy, offline, and regression fixtures pass

promises.statement: A developer can move and export terrain between web and Electron and use it in Godot without manual tile slicing or rule reconstruction.
promises.user: Developer shipping a Godot tilemap from either host
promises.context: A project contains a valid terrain document
promises.trigger: They export generic or Godot terrain output or transfer a project backup
promises.outcome: Both hosts emit identical atlas pixels, mask metadata, terrain credits, and Godot TileSet resource inputs
promises.quality: Archive, atlas, manifest, credits, and Godot metadata hashes match across hosts; output stays offline-capable
promises.recovery: Blocked export names exact terrain issues and produces no partial package; archive conflicts preserve both project versions
promises.evidence: Actual web-to-Electron-to-web project round trip and generic/Godot artifact inspection pass

scenarios.startingState: Character project has no terrain document
scenarios.success: A default grass terrain exists with all masks and a painted preview

scenarios.intent: Paint a small map and see correct edges
scenarios.success: Painting recomputes neighboring masks and visible pixels deterministically

scenarios.actor: Developer matching terrain to a character
scenarios.startingState: Terrain and character share a project
scenarios.success: Terrain colors derive from project theme then allow terrain-local overrides without changing character tokens
Map, material, palette, atlas hash, and character hash reopen unchanged

scenarios.startingState: Project folder or archive contains terrain
scenarios.intent: Continue and export through native locations
scenarios.success: Terrain opens, saves atomically, and exports without renderer filesystem authority
Package contains atlas PNG, cardinal-mask manifest, credits, and optional Godot terrain resource

scenarios.startingState: One host saved a terrain project
scenarios.success: Project and exports round trip with identical terrain and character hashes

scenarios.startingState: Character-only v1/v2 archive or project exists
scenarios.success: Character-only bytes, behavior, exports, packs, and credits remain unchanged

capabilities.statement: User can add one terrain document to an existing project.
User can remove terrain after explicit confirmation without deleting the character recipe.
User can choose grass, dirt, sand, or stone as the generated material.
User can inspect all sixteen cardinal-neighbor autotile variants and their mask IDs.
User can paint and erase cells on a fixed 12 by 8 test map.
User can fill or clear the complete test map.
User can edit terrain surface, detail, edge, and shadow colors.
User can reset terrain colors to values derived from the active project theme.
User can see exact terrain persistence and export blockers.
User can save terrain with the same project revision and snapshot lifecycle.
User can reopen the exact terrain map and atlas pixels.
User can recover terrain through project snapshots, copies, and archive conflict choices.
User can download a generic terrain package with atlas, mask metadata, and credits.
User can download Godot terrain metadata without manual tile slicing.
User can write terrain outputs directly to an approved desktop export folder.
User can transfer terrain in a versioned portable project archive.
User can paint, save, reopen, and export terrain offline after one complete load.

navigation.label: Terrain
navigation.backBehavior: Project and character state remain unchanged

navigation.label: Terrain export
navigation.backBehavior: Returns to terrain with map unchanged

navigation.label: Storage
navigation.backBehavior: Terrain remains in the current project graph

views.name: Terrain empty state
views.purpose: Explain and create the optional second producer

views.name: Terrain workspace
views.purpose: Choose material, paint the map, inspect generated tiles, and recolor terrain

views.name: Remove terrain confirmation
views.purpose: Remove only the terrain document after explicit identity and recovery summary

views.name: Terrain export
views.purpose: Inspect readiness, credits, and produce generic or Godot outputs

views.name: Existing project storage
views.purpose: Save, snapshot, backup, import, copy, and recover the complete graph

expectedBehaviors.oracle: Create terrain adds one schema-version-1 terrain document with grass defaults and leaves the character recipe, lock, pixels, and credits unchanged.
The initial document fills a 12x8 map, displays a nonblank preview, and generates exactly sixteen 32px cardinal-mask tiles in a 128x128 atlas.
Remove opens a confirmation; confirmation creates a normal project snapshot on save, deletes only terrain state, and preserves character state while cancel writes nothing.
Selecting grass, dirt, sand, or stone changes stable material identity, derived defaults, atlas pixels, preview pixels, and credits in one edit.
Toggling a cell changes only that occupancy value and recomputes its cardinal mask plus the four direct neighbors.
Fill makes all 96 cells occupied; Clear makes all cells empty; both are one undoable project edit and never resize the grid.
Atlas and map expose mask IDs 0 through 15 using N=1, E=2, S=4, W=8; identical documents produce identical hashes.
Reset derives four terrain colors deterministically from the active project theme without adding or changing global character token fields.
A valid #RRGGBB terrain color updates atlas and map pixels; invalid text stays visible, reports an adjacent error, and does not mutate committed terrain.
Terrain-local overrides never change character theme values, selected assets, character render hash, or character export hash.
A terrain edit marks Unsaved in one frame and commits through the existing 750ms project revision transaction with a prior snapshot.
Reload or relaunch restores the exact terrain document, map occupancy, atlas hash, map hash, and unchanged character hash.
A stale terrain edit writes nothing and offers the existing reload, overwrite-with-checkpoint, save-copy, or cancel choices.
After one controlled load, terrain paint, theme, save, reopen, backup, and export complete with zero required network requests.
Generic export emits terrain-atlas.png, terrain-manifest.json, build-manifest.json, credits.json, and CREDITS.txt with exact 32px regions for all sixteen masks.
Godot export adds terrain_tileset.tres and README-GODOT-TERRAIN.md whose atlas coordinates, tile size, source ID, and terrain peering bits match the manifest.
Terrain credits name generator source, author, CC0 license, material ID, and project identity with no unselected material.
Invalid palette, schema, or empty required atlas state blocks both commands, names exact issues, and emits no partial download or filesystem output.
Open terrain export preserves the terrain document and shows current readiness, atlas identity, and credits before enabling commands.
Packaged Electron opens archive-v3 or folder terrain through existing opaque project grants with no new renderer path authority.
Desktop save writes terrain.json atomically with the complete graph and fingerprints it for external-change detection.
Desktop generic and Godot terrain exports write only canonical relative entries to the approved export directory and read back successfully.
Terrain edits participate in the existing Save, Discard, or Cancel close contract without a producer-specific bypass.
A terrain project writes archive format 3 with terrain.json in the complete manifest; character-only archives remain byte-compatible v1/v2.
The other host opens terrain only after checksum and strict terrain schema validation and renders identical atlas/map hashes.
Unedited re-export preserves terrain document semantics, character semantics, and all generated terrain output hashes without host metadata.
Archive-v1/v2 readers see literal format 3 and fail with unsupported-version before returning or mutating a project.
Every retained MVP, UPD-001, and UPD-002 character, pack, archive-v1/v2, generic/Godot, offline, and cross-host regression passes unchanged.

flows.completion: Optional terrain is created, inspected, and removable without character drift
Painted map and complete autotile set reflect exact cardinal masks
Terrain matches project mood without changing character semantics

steps.action: Edit terrain colors and correct invalid input

flows.completion: Terrain persists, conflicts safely, and remains usable offline
Generic and Godot terrain packages are complete and credited
Terrain project and outputs use secure native locations

preconditions: Terrain project exists on one host
flows.completion: Terrain round trips with exact semantic and output parity

preconditions: Baseline character fixtures and old reader exist
flows.completion: Terrain is fail-closed for old hosts and invisible to character-only outputs

exclusions.expectation: Install or author external terrain packs
exclusions.reason: UPD-003 must prove the second producer before generalizing pack profile and authoring contracts
exclusions.consequence: Four built-in CC0 procedural materials only
exclusions.disposition: Future update after terrain runtime evidence

exclusions.expectation: Blend arbitrary pairs of materials in one map
exclusions.reason: Transition systems require pair-specific art and corner semantics beyond one cardinal material
exclusions.consequence: One active material per terrain document
exclusions.disposition: Next terrain expansion from real pair fixtures

exclusions.expectation: Generate 47-tile blob, quarter-tile, or Wang sets
exclusions.reason: Sixteen cardinal masks are the smallest complete autotile contract and map directly to Godot peering bits
exclusions.disposition: Versioned terrain profile after measured need

exclusions.expectation: Animate water, foam, waterfalls, or terrain FX
exclusions.reason: Animated terrain introduces timing and multi-frame atlas contracts independent of static autotiling
exclusions.consequence: Water is not one of the first four materials

exclusions.expectation: Scatter flowers, pebbles, cracks, foliage, or variation layers
exclusions.reason: Decorator density and overlap are a second generation system
exclusions.consequence: Tiles use deterministic material texture only

exclusions.expectation: Choose arbitrary map dimensions or import a tilemap
exclusions.reason: A fixed test map proves masks and persistence without becoming a level editor
exclusions.consequence: Preview map is fixed at 12x8 and is not exported as a game level

exclusions.expectation: Place characters, structures, and terrain together in an exportable scene
exclusions.reason: One terrain producer does not yet justify a scene graph
exclusions.consequence: Character and terrain share project/theme but export independently

exclusions.expectation: Generate structures, buildings, props, or vegetation objects
exclusions.reason: Distinct perspective, footprint, anchor, and layer contracts require a separate producer update

exclusions.expectation: Generate normal, specular, emissive, or height maps
exclusions.reason: Material maps depend on manual editing and engine-lighting evidence

exclusions.expectation: Replace character tokens with a universal project token vocabulary
exclusions.reason: Changing the verified six-token schema would alter baseline project bytes before a third producer proves shared semantics
exclusions.consequence: Terrain has a strict local four-color palette derived from project theme

exclusions.expectation: Add Unity, RPG Maker, GameMaker, or another engine adapter
exclusions.reason: Engine target selection and downstream fixtures are independent of terrain generation

exclusions.expectation: Draw or edit terrain pixels manually
exclusions.reason: This increment generates deterministic tiles and does not replace an art editor
exclusions.consequence: Users control material and palette, not individual pixels

workPackages.gate: Complete product, UX, engineering, quality, threat, exclusion, review, approval, and lock artifacts validate
Strict terrain schema, cardinal masks, deterministic renderer, and baseline hash fixtures pass
Optional terrain graph persists transactionally through IndexedDB and Electron project writes with snapshots/conflicts
Archive-v3, old-reader failure, character-only byte compatibility, and cross-host round trip pass
Generic and Godot terrain artifacts are deterministic, complete, credited, and downstream-inspected
All terrain controls, states, recovery, keyboard, reflow, offline, and desktop workflows pass actual UI
All affected MVP, UPD-001, and UPD-002 tests pass with zero severity 0-2 issues and exact character hashes

environment.web: Playwright 1.61.1 Chromium against a test-owned Vite production preview at 1440x900, 640x450, and 320x800 with reduced motion and service-worker-controlled offline restart
environment.desktop: Packaged Electron 40.10.6 Windows x64 portable build on Windows 10.0.26220 with isolated userData, context isolation, sandbox, and frozen 18-method preload bridge
environment.crossHost: Actual production web UI to packaged Electron UI to production web UI using deterministic archive format 3
environment.godot: Godot 4.7.1 stable official a13da4feb headless import and TileSet semantic validation
environment.quality: Vitest 4.1.10 strict schema, 64 material-mask pixel oracle, PNG, export, IndexedDB v4, archive v3, old-reader, rollback, and performance fixtures
environment.pagesSimulation: Exact /the-sprite-project/ service-worker scope with online install, controlled restart, offline reopen, local project, and export

unverifiedReleaseGates: Actual GitHub Pages publication, deployed smoke/offline run, and deployed rollback/withdrawal rehearsal
Independent clean-machine Windows launch, unsigned SmartScreen observation, and executable rollback

decisions.decision: UPD-003 adds one built-in static terrain producer before structures, scenes, or expanded terrain packs. Its fixed 12x8 occupancy map is a bounded verification surface for edges, corners, holes, accessibility, persistence, and performance, not a game-level format.
decisions.evidence: Phase 5 roadmap, MVP OUT-TERRAIN disposition, UPD-002 completion, and direct user authorization on 2026-07-21

decisions.decision: The first terrain profile is a complete sixteen-tile cardinal N=1 E=2 S=4 W=8 mask set at 32px per tile.
decisions.evidence: Smallest complete autotile behavior and direct Godot terrain peering-bit mapping

decisions.decision: Terrain is an optional strict TerrainDocumentV1 in ProjectGraphV2; project.json remains schema version 2 and character recipes remain unchanged.
decisions.evidence: Preserves baseline project fields and lets repositories save one atomic graph

decisions.decision: Terrain projects use .spriteproject archive format 3 with terrain.json; character-only projects continue writing existing format 1 or 2 bytes.
decisions.evidence: Strict older readers must fail before mutation rather than silently drop an unknown producer

decisions.decision: Terrain uses four local color roles derived from the active project theme with local overrides; global six-token character schema is unchanged.
decisions.evidence: Avoids baseline byte/hash drift until another producer proves shared token semantics

decisions.decision: Grass, dirt, sand, and stone are deterministic procedural CC0 materials shipped in code; terrain pack install/authoring is excluded.
decisions.evidence: Proves producer workflow and shared project/export contracts without reopening .spritepack humanoid profile
